design_guidance.designer_notes: Gate rewards with readable affordances so exploration feels fair, not arbitrary.
when_to_avoid: When a linear narrative cannot tolerate optional detours
When map scale cannot support meaningful secrets or rewards
design_guidance.when_to_use: When discovery and player agency are core to the fantasy.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Open worlds, metroidvania maps, optional POI layers, and reward-dense detours.

skills.learning_outcome: Players learn to read environmental cues, map topology, and reward density when choosing where to go next.
skills.name: Exploration
practice_activities: Backtrack after acquiring new traversal abilities
Mark or remember optional paths for later
Balance main objective vs side content
Prioritize landmarks visible from multiple routes
Return to hub before tackling newly unlocked side areas
skills.summary: Discovering routes, secrets, and optional content in a game space.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Match telegraph length to reaction window; scale generously for first encounters.
when_to_avoid: When accessibility requires generous i-frames without telegraphs
When input latency or touch controls cannot support tight reaction windows
design_guidance.when_to_use: When mastery should feel earned through repetition and readable threats.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Boss fights, elite enemies, parry tutorials, and dodge-heavy encounter design.

skills.learning_outcome: Players internalize enemy tell rhythms and commit to actions at the right moment.
skills.name: Combat Timing
practice_activities: Dodge through telegraphed attacks
Punish recovery frames
Chain light attacks without over-committing
Practice parry timing on slow telegraphs first
Learn i-frame dodge distance per attack type
skills.summary: Executing attacks, dodges, and counters within telegraphed windows.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Introduce patterns one at a time before combinations.
when_to_avoid: When randomness should dominate over learnable patterns
When accessibility needs simplified single-phase encounters
design_guidance.when_to_use: When mastery depends on learning repeating enemy, boss, or puzzle patterns.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Boss rushes, puzzle floors, telegraphed combat curricula.

skills.learning_outcome: Players build mental models of systems and predict upcoming states.
skills.name: Pattern Recognition
practice_activities: Learn boss phase transitions
Decode weakness cycles
Map puzzle rule interactions
Identify safe zones during repeating bullet patterns
Note phase-shift audio or visual cues before combos
skills.summary: Identifying repeating enemy behaviors, puzzle rules, or boss phases.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Make scarcity readable on HUD at all times.
when_to_avoid: When power fantasy requires abundant consumables without tradeoffs
When onboarding should hide economy systems until mid-game
design_guidance.when_to_use: When limited heals, currency, or slots force planning across encounters.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Roguelike routes, survival loops, shop-before-boss decisions.

skills.learning_outcome: Players weigh short-term survival against long-term progression.
skills.name: Resource Management
practice_activities: Spend currency before checkpoints
Reserve healing for boss rooms
Choose loadout within slot limits
Track earn rate versus upcoming shop prices
Hoard key items until multiple locks appear
skills.summary: Allocating limited resources across encounters, runs, or sessions.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Landmarks should be visible from multiple angles.
when_to_avoid: When levels are isolated rooms without meaningful interconnection
When instant fast travel removes the need for route planning
design_guidance.when_to_use: When interconnected maps reward mental mapping and route optimization.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Metroidvania worlds, hubs with portals, backtracking routes.

skills.learning_outcome: Players develop mental maps and optimize traversal after gaining new abilities.
skills.name: Spatial Navigation
practice_activities: Revisit areas with new movement tools
Use map UI to plan routes
Shortcut through unlocked doors
Memorize shortcut unlocks after gaining new abilities
Compare route length with and without new traversal tools
skills.summary: Orienting in interconnected spaces and planning efficient routes.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Expose failure cost before committing to risky nodes.
when_to_avoid: Onboarding where failure cost is opaque
When punishment for curiosity undermines the core fantasy
design_guidance.when_to_use: When players choose whether to push optional risk for upside rewards.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Roguelike pathing, optional elites, low-health shop visits.

skills.learning_outcome: Players calibrate aggression against failure cost and reward upside.
skills.name: Risk Evaluation
practice_activities: Enter optional combat rooms for rewards
Retreat to bench before boss attempts
Choose roguelike paths with unknown rewards
Compare reward preview against current health or lives
Abort optional challenges when build is misaligned
skills.summary: Judging whether to push forward, retreat, or spend limited resources.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Tag synergies without auto-solving optimal picks.
when_to_avoid: When build identity should remain fixed per character or class
When synergy complexity overwhelms new players in the first act
design_guidance.when_to_use: When run identity emerges from offered upgrade choices.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Boon picks, card drafts, charm loadouts.

skills.learning_outcome: Players evaluate synergies and constraints when composing a build.
skills.name: Deck Building
practice_activities: Pick boons that complement weapon aspect
Avoid conflicting modifiers
Plan around slot or notch limits
Identify core synergy tags before picking passives
Remove dead picks when draft offers direct upgrades
skills.summary: Assembling synergistic ability sets from offered choices.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Ensure evidence is citeable in voice or text chat.
when_to_avoid: When voice or text discussion is unavailable for async sessions
When player count is too low for meaningful accusation loops
design_guidance.when_to_use: When hidden roles and group discussion resolve conflict.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Meeting phases, voting, accusation UI.

skills.learning_outcome: Players practice reading social signals and constructing evidence-based accusations.
skills.name: Social Deduction
practice_activities: Track player movements on mental map
Cross-reference task completion claims
Present timing evidence in meetings
Note alibis that contradict observed paths
Vote only when the evidence chain is complete
skills.summary: Inferring hidden roles from behavior, alibis, and group discussion.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Offer assist or practice modes without shaming players.
when_to_avoid: When narrative or accessibility should not gate progress on frame-perfect input
When inconsistent frame pacing undermines jump and dodge timing
design_guidance.when_to_use: When fine motor timing is the primary skill test.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Platform gauntlets, bullet patterns, dodge windows.

skills.learning_outcome: Players refine input timing and spatial judgment under pressure.
skills.name: Precision Movement
practice_activities: Clear platform gauntlets
Dodge bullet patterns
Maintain momentum through chained jumps
Practice jump timing on static hazards before moving ones
Use short respawn loops to drill one hard segment
skills.summary: Executing tight platforming or dodge windows with fine motor control.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Foreshadow consequences within two interactions when possible.
when_to_avoid: When story is explicitly told without ambiguity or player interpretation
When consequences must remain opaque for horror or mystery tone
design_guidance.when_to_use: When story outcomes depend on reading dialogue and environmental clues.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Branching dialogue, investigation boards, lore items.

skills.learning_outcome: Players connect story beats to mechanics and predict consequence branches.
skills.name: Narrative Inference
practice_activities: Weigh dialogue choices with known character goals
Infer lore from item descriptions
Predict outcomes from faction relationships
Track faction reputation changes after key choices
Cross-reference environmental props with dialogue hints
skills.summary: Drawing conclusions from dialogue, environmental storytelling, and player choices.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Preview upcoming area threats to inform loadout choices.
when_to_avoid: When all equipment should remain viable without meaningful tradeoffs
When lengthy pre-fight menus break combat pacing
design_guidance.when_to_use: When slot limits require cutting options before challenges.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Pre-boss benches, equipment menus, charm notches.

skills.learning_outcome: Players trade off power spikes against flexibility for upcoming content.
skills.name: Loadout Optimization
practice_activities: Swap charms before boss fights
Equip elemental weapons for weaknesses
Balance offense vs survivability slots
Swap to elemental gear when weakness hints appear
Drop situational charms for generalist boss kits
skills.summary: Selecting equipment, charms, or abilities within capacity constraints.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Node icons must be learnable within one act.
when_to_avoid: When sessions are short and fixed-length without branching routes
When procedural maps hide node information entirely until entry
design_guidance.when_to_use: When procedural routes demand mid-run path decisions.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Act maps, chamber doors, node graphs.

skills.learning_outcome: Players evaluate path risk, reward type, and build synergy mid-run.
skills.name: Run Planning
practice_activities: Choose chamber type in roguelike routes
Skip shops when low on currency
Target god boons for build direction
Favor reward nodes that advance current build tags
Skip combat chambers when low on recovery resources
skills.summary: Routing through procedural or branching sessions to maximize rewards.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Reward scanning without mandatory slow-walk everywhere.
when_to_avoid: When action-first pacing cannot afford scouting or observation beats
When UI already surfaces all hidden information without player effort
design_guidance.when_to_use: When observation before action prevents costly mistakes.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Stealth loops, deduction phases, scouting open worlds.

skills.learning_outcome: Players prioritize observation and note-taking over immediate reaction.
skills.name: Information Gathering
practice_activities: Scan rooms for task progress
Observe enemy patrol routes
Read UI feedback before spending resources
Pause before entering new zones to scan patrol routes
Review quest log clues before spending currency
skills.summary: Collecting clues from the environment before committing to action.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Show regen delay clearly after exhaustive actions.
when_to_avoid: When traversal fantasy should never be limited by exertion meters
When stamina tax frustrates exploration more than it adds tension
design_guidance.when_to_use: When sustained actions deplete a regenerating exertion pool.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Climbing, sprinting, combo-heavy combat.

skills.learning_outcome: Players pace climbs, sprints, and attacks to avoid exhaustion penalties.
skills.name: Stamina Management
practice_activities: Climb surfaces in planned segments
Rest on ledges during traversal puzzles
Alternate heavy attacks with recovery
Plan climb routes with mid-way ledge rests
Pace sprint bursts between cover in open areas
skills.summary: Balancing sustained actions against recovery periods.

design_guidance.designer_notes: Wrong-tool feedback should educate, not hard-lock.
when_to_avoid: When enemies should be defeated without explicit counter systems
When mid-fight tool swapping breaks combat flow or readability
design_guidance.when_to_use: When targets have explicit counters players must discover or equip.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Boss weakness cycles, elemental shields, tool-gated obstacles.

skills.learning_outcome: Players experiment with tools and observe feedback until optimal damage appears.
skills.name: Weakness Exploitation
practice_activities: Cycle weapons against boss immunities
Acquire keys before attempting locks
Use elemental counters in combat
Test weapon types against shielded enemies in safe rooms
Stock counter items before entering boss arenas
skills.summary: Identifying and applying the correct counter to a resistant target.

skills.name: Aim Precision
skills.summary: Stabilizing reticles and leading targets to land shots under pressure.
skills.learning_outcome: Players control recoil, track movement, and commit shots within effective range windows.
practice_activities: Practice tracking strafing targets at medium range
Tap-fire for recoil control before full-auto bursts
Pre-aim common angles before peeking cover
Lead projectile shots on moving targets

design_guidance.when_to_use: When hit confirmation and target tracking are primary skill tests.
design_guidance.where_to_use: FPS/TPS arenas, twin-stick shooters, and precision weapon tiers.
when_to_avoid: When aim assist must fully automate targeting for accessibility
When projectile travel time cannot be read clearly
design_guidance.designer_notes: Expose readable hit feedback and optional practice ranges.

skills.name: Combo Execution
skills.summary: Linking attacks and cancels within timing windows for extended pressure.
skills.learning_outcome: Players memorize routes and execute inputs without breaking chain rules.
practice_activities: Drill bread-and-butter strings on training dummies
Practice cancel windows from normals into specials
Punish whiffed enemy attacks with max-damage routes
Adapt routes when resources or meter are low

design_guidance.when_to_use: When mastery depth should scale through attack linking.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Fighting games, action combat, and boss rush modes.
when_to_avoid: When input complexity would block onboarding in a narrative-first game
When latency makes link timing unreliable
design_guidance.designer_notes: Teach one link at a time; show frame advantage on hit.

skills.name: Turn Order Planning
skills.summary: Sequencing actions across units or turns to maximize tempo and resources.
skills.learning_outcome: Players forecast initiative, setup turns, and spend actions before windows close.
practice_activities: Plan buffs before burst damage turns
Hold interrupts for enemy charge attacks
Rotate party roles to cover weakness phases
Bank resources for boss vulnerability windows

design_guidance.when_to_use: When turn structure creates planning puzzles each round.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Tactics RPGs, ATB systems, and party-based boss fights.
when_to_avoid: When real-time chaos is the core fantasy
When turn UI obscures upcoming enemy intents
design_guidance.designer_notes: Surface turn order and telegraphed enemy intents clearly.

skills.name: Territory Control
skills.summary: Claiming, holding, and contesting map space for strategic advantage.
skills.learning_outcome: Players read map pressure, rotate to objectives, and deny enemy resources.
practice_activities: Track minion wave timing before objective contests
Trade small objectives for high-value map control
Ward or scout fog-of-war before committing
Collapse on overextended opponents as a team

design_guidance.when_to_use: When map ownership changes win conditions over time.
design_guidance.where_to_use: MOBAs, RTS lanes, zone-shrink battle royales.
when_to_avoid: When matches should remain purely duel-focused without map layers
When UI cannot communicate contested objectives
design_guidance.designer_notes: Make ownership states readable at a glance on the minimap.

skills.name: Team Coordination
skills.summary: Aligning roles, timing, and communication to achieve group objectives.
skills.learning_outcome: Players assign roles, call plays, and synchronize ability timing with allies.
practice_activities: Assign focus targets before engagements
Time crowd control with ally burst windows
Rotate as a squad when objectives spawn
Share resource pickups based on role priority

design_guidance.when_to_use: When success depends on complementary player roles.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Co-op horde modes, MOBA teams, and asymmetric multiplayer.
when_to_avoid: When solo play cannot be supported at all
When voice comms are unavailable and pinging is too weak
design_guidance.designer_notes: Provide non-verbal callouts and role clarity in UI.

skills.name: Crafting Planning
skills.summary: Gathering inputs and scheduling crafts to meet upcoming challenges.
skills.learning_outcome: Players forecast material needs and pipeline crafts before deadlines.
practice_activities: Stock consumables before biome shifts
Chain refinery steps to avoid idle stations
Prioritize tools that unlock new resource nodes
Balance crafting time against daylight or hazard timers

design_guidance.when_to_use: When crafting loops gate exploration or combat readiness.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Survival sandboxes, RPG crafting, and colony sims.
when_to_avoid: When instant loot should fully replace player-side production
When recipe trees overwhelm early sessions
design_guidance.designer_notes: Stage recipe discovery near relevant biomes or bosses.

skills.name: Relationship Building
skills.summary: Investing time and gifts to grow bonds that unlock story and perks.
skills.learning_outcome: Players track preferences, schedule social time, and avoid relationship decay.
practice_activities: Gift items matching character preferences
Attend scheduled events before deadlines
Balance farm work with social visits each day
Resolve heart-event prompts with prior lore knowledge

design_guidance.when_to_use: When long-term NPC bonds are a primary progression axis.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Life sims, dating sim layers, and faction reputation systems.
when_to_avoid: When narrative should remain impersonal or purely systemic
When missed events hard-lock content without recovery
design_guidance.designer_notes: Telegraph gift preferences and event windows on calendar UI.

skills.name: Tension Management
skills.summary: Regulating exposure to threat while pursuing objectives under fear pressure.
skills.learning_outcome: Players ration light, ammo, and safe routes while monitoring escalating danger.
practice_activities: Listen for audio cues before entering new rooms
Retreat to safe rooms when resource pools are low
Route around optional haunts when objective is near
Use distraction tools to break pursuit loops

design_guidance.when_to_use: When fear and scarcity combine to pressure decision-making.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Horror survival, stealth horror, and limited-save campaigns.
when_to_avoid: When power fantasy requires constant aggressive empowerment
When jumpscares replace readable threat buildup
design_guidance.designer_notes: Give players reliable relief valves after sustained tension.

skills.name: Rhythm Timing
skills.summary: Executing inputs on musical beats and charted note lanes.
skills.learning_outcome: Players internalize tempo, anticipate chart density, and recover from miss chains.
practice_activities: Practice slow charts before high-density sections
Memorize recurring fill patterns in boss choruses
Alternate lanes without breaking combo multiplier
Use audio cues when visual indicators lag

design_guidance.when_to_use: When music-driven timing is the core interaction loop.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Rhythm action, dance pads, and chart-driven mini-games.
when_to_avoid: When music licensing cannot support consistent beat mapping
When input latency varies widely across platforms
design_guidance.designer_notes: Calibrate note windows per platform; offer offset calibration.

skills.name: Puzzle Logic
skills.summary: Deducing rules and manipulating systems to reach valid puzzle states.
skills.learning_outcome: Players hypothesize rules, test interactions, and chain deductions across rooms.
practice_activities: Encode and test one rule change at a time
Map symbol interactions before committing moves
Backtrack with new information from partial solutions
Explain the rule aloud before executing long chains

design_guidance.when_to_use: When player-driven deduction is the primary reward.
design_guidance.where_to_use: Logic puzzles, rule-bending games, and investigation boards.
when_to_avoid: When trial-and-error would feel arbitrary without feedback
When puzzles gate urgent action pacing without opt-out
design_guidance.designer_notes: Give incremental feedback for partial correct states.
